During the season, they competed in the [[Super League XXIX]], the [[2024 Challenge Cup]], and the [[2024 World Club Challenge]]. The 2024 season saw Wigan complete the quadruple, the club's second in its history after the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]], and only the fourth modern quadruple in British rugby league. In [[Sky Sports]] end of season review, Wigan were praised for their academy, coaching setup, squad hunger, and utilisation of [[Bevan French]].{{Cite news|last=Wellens|first=Megan|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13231913/the-wigan-warriors-dynasty-what-makes-the-super-league-champions-tick|title=The Wigan Warriors dynasty: What makes the Super League champions tick?|work=Sky Sports|date=13 October 2024|access-date=13 October 2024}} [[The Guardian]] described the 2024 squad as one of the all time greats.{{Cite news |last=Bower |first=Aaron |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/oct/13/now-one-of-rugby-leagues-greatest-sides-what-next-for-wigan-warriors |title=Now one of rugby league's greatest sides, what next for Wigan Warriors? |work=The Guardian |location=London |date=13 October 2024 |access-date=13 October 2024}} The 2024 Wigan team won the [[BBC Sports Team of the Year Award]], seeing the club win for a second time after their first in 1994.https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4gpe4gy3pyo ==Preseason friendlies== {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and |access-date=12 July 2024 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} |} ==Super League== {{main|Super League XXIX}} ===Regular season=== Wigan Warriors claimed the [[League Leaders' Shield]] on the final day of the 2024 regular season,{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2y8n84z0eo|title=Wigan thrash Salford to retain League Leaders' Shield |date=19 September 2024|work=BBC Sport|access-date=22 September 2024}} and saw four players make the [[Super League Dream Team]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c78de9v0q21o |title=Wigan head up Super League Dream Team selections |date=22 September 2024|work=BBC Sport|access-date=22 September 2024}} ====Fixtures==== {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV{{efn|Ahead of 2024, [[Sky Sports]] announced they would be broadcasting all Super League matches over the next three seasons.{{Cite news |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12984274/sky-sports-to-show-every-super-league-match-live-as-part-of-historic-new-three-year-tv-rights-deal |title=Sky Sports to show every Super League match live as part of historic new three-year TV rights deal|work=Sky Sports|access-date=7 September 2024}} Exact TV channels can be sourced by using [https://web.archive.org/ WayBack Machine] on [https://www.rugbyleagueontv.com/ RugbyLeagueOnTV].
''NB'' Not all games were broadcast on Sky Sports TV (advertised as "Sky Sports Exclusives"), the remainder were broadcast using Sky Sports+ (from it's launch in August), their on demand services accessible online or via red button. These games were also made available on Super League+, and a select few on the [[BBC]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68229917 |title=Super League: BBC to show live games for first time after signing three-year broadcast deal|date=7 February 2024|work=BBC Sport|access-date=7 September 2024}} Further, games on Main Event were also broadcast on Action or
